name: Heli Spetziotiko (Eel Spetzes-style)
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds Eel, (moray, conger, or freshwater eel), dressed, cut into 2-1/2 inches thick pieces
3 Sun-dried tomatoes (in oil), snipped up and soaked in:
3/4 pound Tomatoes, skinned, seeded and chopped
1 tablespoon Fresh thyme, or 1/2 teaspoon Greek "Mountain" thyme
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy skillet or wide flameproof casserole, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the eel pieces in batches until well browned on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Lower the heat to medium and add another tablespoon of olive oil if needed. Sauté the onions gently until translucent.
3. Pound the sun-dried tomatoes (if using) into a paste using the back of a spoon or pestle.
4. Add the chopped tomatoes, honey, thyme, bay leaf, lemon zest, garlic, parsley, mint, salt, and pepper to the pan. Stir to combine.
5. Return the eel pieces to the sauce and stir gently.
6. Transfer the mixture to a baking dish or earthenware casserole. Sprinkle with crumbled feta cheese and lemon juice.
7. Bake in an oven preheated to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the eel is cooked through and the sauce has thickened slightly.
